one_time_description: Direct charge to accumulated surplus due to business combination — dissolution of Fort Providence Resource Management Board, a 100% owned subsidiary, resulting in net liabilities assumed of $37,355
economic_notes: Subsequent to year end, signed CMHC agreement to renovate 6 properties and construct 4 additional properties for maximum $4,795,598. Evergreen Forestry Limited Partnership provides forest fire fighting and forestry services. Digaa Enterprises Ltd. provides construction services and commercial/residential property rental. 84% of funding from three sources (Deh Cho First Nation, Government of Canada, GNWT).
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2021 (not 2024). Province is NT (Northwest Territories). Audit opinion is QUALIFIED. Nation is located in Fort Providence, Northwest Territories. One fully consolidated subsidiary (Undah Gogha Corporation 100%); one GBE on modified equity basis (Digaa Enterprises Ltd. 70%); one government business partnership on modified equity basis (Evergreen Forestry Limited Partnership 50%). Fort Providence Resource Management Board dissolved March 31, 2021 via business combination resulting in direct charge to accumulated surplus of $37,355. Significant COVID-19 emergency funding received. No remuneration schedule found in extracted text. OSR calculated as total revenue $2,853,443 minus total government transfers $2,782,057 = $71,386. Auditor firm name not identified in extracted text. CMHC housing agreement signed subsequent to year end for up to $4,795,598 to renovate 6 and construct 4 new properties.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
